task.title: Setting compiler options for show dependencies and remote syntax check
task.shortdesc: To use the show dependencies and remote syntax check functions, you must set up appropriate compiler options.

keywords.indexterm: show dependenciessetting compiler optionssetting assembler options
remote syntax checksetting compiler optionssetting assembler options
PL/I Settingsshow dependencies and remote syntax check
COBOL Settingsshow dependencies and remote syntax check
Assembler Settingsshow dependencies and remote syntax check

taskbody.context:
provides show dependencies and remote syntax check functions that detect dependencies for COBOL, PL/I, and Assembler programs. These functions use the COBOL and PL/I compilers and to locate the copy members, include files, and macros that are needed to compile a program properly. These functions are available from a number of locations in the interface:
From the view, select a local or remote COBOL or PL/I file or a remote Assembler file, or a local project that contains COBOL or PL/I files and then select Show Dependencies. The Show Dependencies action is not supported for sequential data sets.
From the Work Offline wizard, select Show Dependencies on the Show Dependencies for Selected Resources page.
From the view or an editor, select or open a COBOL, PL/I, or Assembler file and then select Remote Syntax Check or press Ctrl+Alt+R.

steps.stepsection: To set compiler options for a show dependencies or remote syntax check operation, do these steps:

step.cmd: On the Procedures and Steps page of the COBOL, PL/I, or Assembler tab in the property group editor, expand the ELAXFCOC, ELAXFPL1, or ELAXFASM procedure name.
Double-click the COBOL, PLI, or ASM step.
Select the Support Error Feedback check box.
step.info: This checkbox prompts the compiler to allocate an XML file during show dependencies or remote syntax check operations. The show dependencies and syntax check operations parse this XML file to display dependent files.

step.cmd: In the Data Set Qualifier for Compiler Errors field, type a qualifier to which you have access to allocate data sets.
step.info: This data set qualifier is used as a prefix for the names of the data sets that are allocated during the syntax check, show dependencies, and rebuild process.

taskbody.postreq: If show dependencies or remote syntax check requests do not succeed after you set these options, check that the TSO command server is configured properly. A simple way to check this server is to allocate a data set from the view or Remote Systems view.

link.linktext: Determining the source of generated JCL
